title: lycoctonum × fudjisanense
intro: Lycoctonum × fudjisanense, also known as Lycoctonum fudjisanense or Fudjisan Lycoctonum, is a rare, hybrid plant species in the Berberidaceae family, resulting from the cross between Lycoctonum alpinum and Coptis japonica. The plant contains berberine, a bioactive compound with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which is also responsible for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Lycoctonum × fudjisanense include its potential to treat gastrointestinal disorders, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activities, making it a valuable resource in traditional Chinese medicine. Herbal preparations of Lycoctonum × fudjisanense include dried roots, which are often used to make teas or tinctures, and may also be used in combination with other medicinal herbs to enhance their effects.
